data.title: Spot silver rose more than $28 in the first month of the new year, a cumulative increase of nearly 40%.
data.description: Following APMEX (American Precious Metals Exchange), brokerage trading platforms showed that spot silver prices reached $100 per ounce, a nearly 4% increase on the day, and a cumulative increase of over $28 in the first month of the new year, representing a nearly 40% increase. Since the end of November last year, spot silver prices have almost doubled, with the upward trend accelerating significantly in the past two months. (Jinshi)

data.title: After reducing some of his long ETH positions, Brother Machi deposited 250,000 USDC into Hyperliquid to continue increasing his holdings.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, after ETH fell below $2,900, Machi reduced some of his long ETH positions to avoid liquidation. He then deposited 250,000 USDC into Hyperliquid to increase his long positions. He currently holds 4,450 ETH, with a liquidation price of $2,841.97.

data.title: Binance Futures will launch SPACEUSDT and FIGHTUSDT perpetual contracts.
data.description: According to the official announcement, Binance Futures will launch SPACEUSDT and FIGHTUSDT perpetual contracts.

data.title: A whale deposited 1.82 million USDC into Hyperliquid to open long positions in BTC, ETH, and HYPE, with the current position valued at $25.9 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale deposited 1.82 million USDC into Hyperliquid over the past 3 days to open long positions in BTC, ETH, and HYPE, with a current position value of $25.9 million. This includes: a 40x leveraged Bitcoin long position of 200.67 BTC ($17.79 million), a 25x leveraged Ethereum long position of 2000 ETH ($5.81 million), and a 10x leveraged HYPE long position of 104,319 HYPE ($2.28 million).

data.title: New York silver futures broke through $100/ounce, setting a new all-time high.
data.description: New York silver futures broke through $100/ounce, hitting a new all-time high, up 3.76% on the day. (Jinshi)

data.title: The release of leading U.S. economic indicators was delayed due to a temporary network outage by The Conference Board.
data.description: The release of the leading U.S. economic indicators has been delayed due to a temporary network outage caused by the Conference Board. The indicators will be released as soon as network connectivity is restored. (Jinshi)

data.title: Release of U.S. Leading Economic Indicators Delayed Due to Network Disruption
data.description: The release of the U.S. leading economic indicators has been postponed due to a temporary network disruption at the Conference Board. According to Odaily, the index will be published as soon as the network connection is restored.

data.title: Huang Licheng Reduces ETH Long Position Amid Losses
data.description: On January 23, Huang Licheng, known as 'Machi Big Brother,' has reduced his Ethereum long position, according to BlockBeats. Hyperinsight monitoring reveals that Huang is currently leveraging 25 times to go long on 3,600 ETH, with an average entry price of $2,945.42. The position is experiencing a floating loss of 28%, approximately $110,000, with a liquidation price set at $2,880.36.

data.title: U.S. January Inflation Rate Expectations Revised to 4%
data.description: The final expectation for the United States' one-year inflation rate in January has been adjusted to 4%. According to PANews, this figure is slightly lower than the anticipated 4.2% and the previous value of 4.20%. The revision reflects changes in economic forecasts and market conditions.

data.title: Trump Talks ‘Crypto Capital’ At Davos As Bitcoin Defies Scrutiny
data.description:
Trump's Davos speech fuels the view that institutional players are engineering “investor fatigue” ahead of the next leg-up.
source: https://dailycoin.com/trump-talks-crypto-capital-at-davos-as-bitcoin-defies-scrutiny/?utm_source=cryptopanic&utm_medium=rss

data.title: Standard Chartered analyst: Buying ETH and Bitmine before the end of this week may be a good choice.
data.description: Standard Chartered analyst Geoff Kendrick suggests that buying ETH and Bitmine before the end of this week could be a good option. Increased Ethereum trading activity and Tom Lee's continued buying are positive signs for the cryptocurrency, which has recently fallen sharply from its 2026 highs. Ethereum's base network activity has surged in recent weeks, with transaction volume reaching all-time highs, primarily due to capacity increases from the December Fusaka upgrade. Furthermore, the macroeconomic environment has also played a positive role, with the rising likelihood of Rick Rieder, BlackRock's head of fixed income, becoming the next Federal Reserve chairman, all of which are favorable for risk assets. (CoinDesk)

data.title: A whale unstaking 665,035 HYPE tokens and deposited them into the CEX.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale unstaking 665,035 HYPE tokens, worth $14.23 million, and deposited them into the cryptocurrency exchange Bybit. He then withdrew these 665,035 HYPE tokens from Bybit to another wallet (0x471b) and sold them at an average price of $21.27, receiving 14.14 million USDC in return.

data.title: The final reading for the US one-year inflation rate in January was 4%, compared to an expected 4.2%.
data.description: The final reading for the US one-year inflation rate in January was 4%, below the expected 4.2% and the previous reading of 4.20%. The final reading for the US five- to ten-year inflation rate in January was 3.3%, below the expected 3.4% and the previous reading of 3.40%. (Jinshi)

data.title: First SEC-Approved Spot Dogecoin ETF Debuts on Nasdaq
data.description:
The U. S. crypto ETF landscape expanded again on January 22, 2026, with the launch of the 21Shares Dogecoin ETF (TDOG) on the Nasdaq. Issued by 21Shares, this product marks a historic first: it is the only spot Dogecoin ETF to receive formal approval from the U
source: https://www.ethnews.com/first-sec-approved-spot-dogecoin-etf-debuts-on-nasdaq/

data.title: U.S. January PMI Data Shows Mixed Results
data.description: The preliminary data for the U.S. January S&P Global Services PMI stands at 52.5, slightly below the expected 52.8, and unchanged from the previous value of 52.5. According to Odaily, the S&P Global Manufacturing PMI for January is initially recorded at 51.9, just under the anticipated 52, but an increase from the prior 51.8. Meanwhile, the S&P Global Composite PMI for January is reported at 52.8, falling short of the forecasted 53, yet slightly higher than the previous 52.7.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Drops Below 2,900 USDT with a 2.46% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jan 23, 2026, 14:40 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has dropped below 2,900 USDT and is now trading at 2,898.719971 USDT, with a narrowed 2.46% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Ethereum Falls Below 2900 USDT Amid Daily Decline
data.description: Ethereum has experienced a drop below the 2900 USDT mark, currently trading at 2896.65 USDT. According to Foresight News, this represents a daily decrease of 1.89%.

data.title: Over $27 million in liquidations occurred across the entire network in the past hour, with $10.62 million in ETH liquidations.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, $27.374 million in positions were liquidated across the network in the past hour, including $25.5996 million in long positions and $1.7744 million in short positions. In addition, $10.62 million in ETH positions were liquidated and $5.68 million in BTC positions were liquidated.

data.title: The preliminary reading of the S&P Global Services PMI for January in the United States was 52.5, below the expected 52.8.
data.description: The US S&P Global Services PMI preliminary reading for January was 52.5, below the expected 52.8 and the previous reading of 52.5. The US S&P Global Manufacturing PMI preliminary reading for January was 51.9, below the expected 52 and the previous reading of 51.8. (Jinshi)

data.title: Ethereum Developers Face Challenges in Glamsterdam Upgrade Timeline
data.description:
Ethereum developers are confronting significant challenges in aligning the scope and timeline for the Glamsterdam upgrade, according to PANews. During the 173rd Ethereum consensus layer core developers meeting, it was acknowledged that substantial reductions in the upgrade's scale might be necessary to meet the mid-year launch target. Although developers have not explicitly confirmed a mismatch between the upgrade's scope and the proposed timeline, they recognize the potential issue and aim to resolve it by the end of February.

The meeting discussed the possibility of removing EIP 7732 from Glamsterdam if an interoperable version cannot be achieved by late February, postponing it to a future hard fork. Additionally, updates on other developments were shared: Consensys' Teku client team reported node performance issues discovered during mainnet stress testing, which are being addressed and have delayed their participation in Glamsterdam. EIP 7928 requires minor API change reviews by consensus layer clients, while the deadline for submitting the Headliner EIP proposal for the Hegota upgrade is February 4. Some client teams have completed preliminary work on library updates to support EIP-7688.
